_provenance.note: Snapshot of the ethereum.org bug bounty severity rubric used as the speca-cli wizard default. Refresh by re-running the WebFetch against the source URL and updating fetched_at.

in_scope_components: Execution Layer clients: Besu, Erigon, Geth, Nethermind, Reth
Consensus Layer clients: Lighthouse, Lodestar, Nimbus, Teku, Prysm, Grandine
out_of_scope_components: Infrastructure operated outside the Ethereum protocol
scope_notes: Severity is anchored on validator slashing percentage and network-wide impact.
Bounty figures are upper bounds; actual payout depends on triage.

Critical.criteria: Slash >50% of validators, create infinite ETH, steal or burn ETH from all EOAs, or take down the entire network.
examples: Consensus break that allows arbitrary state mutation
Cryptographic flaw enabling forged validator signatures at scale
Critical.impact: Catastrophic, unrecoverable network-wide failure or value loss.

High.criteria: Slash >33% of validators, trivially cause network splits affecting >33% of the network, or bring down >33% of the network via a single onchain transaction.
examples: Cross-client consensus bug producing a >33% split
Single-transaction crash affecting a third of clients
High.impact: Severe network disruption with significant validator loss.

Medium.criteria: Slash >1% of validators, trivially cause splits affecting >5% of the network, or bring down >5% of the network via a single packet or transaction.
examples: Client-specific crash reachable by a malformed gossip message
Liveness bug affecting a notable share of validators
Medium.impact: Material disruption recoverable by client patches.

Low.criteria: Slash >0.01% of validators, trivially cause splits affecting >0.01% of the network, or bring down >0.01% of the network via a single packet or transaction.
Low.impact: Limited disruption with negligible validator slashing.

Informational.criteria: Issues with no exploitable security impact: documentation gaps, minor code quality, defense-in-depth suggestions.
examples: Hardening recommendation without a concrete attack
Spec ambiguity that does not change client behaviour
Informational.impact: No direct security impact; tracked for awareness only.
